title: Ida cinnabarina
intro: Ida cinnabarina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Ida cinnabarina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ida cinnabarina is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and boost the immune system.

This orchid species is characterized by its unique and colorful flowers, which are a key feature for identification. As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Ida cinnabarina is closely related to other orchid species.
